candidates.title: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability
candidates.field_a: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics — Mpemba effect spectral theory
candidates.field_c: Neurodegenerative protein biochemistry — amyloid aggregation selectivity
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'Mpemba effect protein aggregation amyloid misfolding'. PubMed 'Mpemba effect biology' returned 3 results, none related (author surname match, water anomaly). Semantic Scholar: 5 results for cross-field query, ALL single-field protein aggregation papers — none cite Mpemba effect, anomalous relaxation, or eigenmode-overlap diagnostics. Markov state models are used in BOTH fields independently but the Mpemba eigenmode framework has not been imported into aggregation research.
candidates.related_but_distinct: Markov state models for protein folding/aggregation (Noe, Pande groups) use spectral decomposition but NOT for Mpemba-type anomalous relaxation predictions. The enzymatic Mpemba effect paper (2024 arXiv) addresses enzyme kinetics, not aggregation.
candidates.bridge_novelty: HIGH — the Mpemba index (overlap with slow eigenmodes) as aggregation predictor is entirely novel

candidates.title: Anesthetic Lipid Raft Signaling Paradigm Explains Bacterial Sub-Lethal Antimicrobial Sensing
candidates.field_a: Membrane biophysics / anesthesiology — PLD2 relocalization cascade
candidates.field_c: Bacterial membrane biology — FMM-mediated AMP detection
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'anesthesia PLD2 phospholipase D2 bacterial flotillin membrane microdomain'. Semantic Scholar: 5 results for cross-field query — all bacterial FMM papers (flotillin, MRSA, membrane microdomain). None cite Hansen lab PLD2 anesthesia work or the GM1 raft disruption signaling cascade. Garcia-Fernandez 2017 (Cell) shows FMM disruption inhibits MRSA resistance but uses a completely different mechanism framework (not PLD-mediated signaling).
candidates.related_but_distinct: Garcia-Fernandez 2017 demonstrates FMM disruption as antibiotic mechanism but the signaling TRANSDUCTION pathway (how disruption is sensed) remains uncharacterized. The anesthesia PLD2 cascade provides the missing signaling template. Ukleja et al. 2024 (Nature Comms) shows flotillin stabilizes unfolded proteins in FMMs — confirms FMM functional importance but doesn't address disruption sensing.
candidates.bridge_novelty: HIGH — the specific PLD2-relocalization-to-signaling-lipid cascade from anesthesia has never been proposed for bacterial FMM disruption sensing

candidates.title: High-Entropy Alloy Mixing Thermodynamics Predicts Gut Microbiome Functional Synergy
candidates.field_a: Materials science — HEA thermodynamics, Hume-Rothery rules
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'high entropy alloy microbiome gut community diversity synergy'. Semantic Scholar: 5 results for cross-field query — all single-field (HEA thermodynamics papers OR microbiome entropy paper, zero overlap). Fassler et al. 2025 uses relative entropy for microbiome functional redundancy — different framework (information theory, not mixing thermodynamics). No paper applies Hume-Rothery rules, cocktail effect, or ΔG_mix formalism to microbial communities.
candidates.related_but_distinct: Thermodynamic frameworks for microbial ecology exist (PLOS Comp Biol 2021: free-energy constraints on community assembly) but use chemical thermodynamics of metabolic reactions, NOT materials science mixing thermodynamics (configurational entropy, lattice distortion, Hume-Rothery rules). The mathematical identity between configurational entropy and Shannon diversity has been noted in information theory but never used to transfer HEA predictive criteria to ecology.
candidates.bridge_novelty: HIGH — Hume-Rothery miscibility rules as community assembly criteria is entirely novel

candidates.title: Earthquake ETAS Self-Exciting Point Process Models Tumor Recurrence Dynamics
candidates.field_c: Clinical oncology — tumor dormancy and recurrence timing
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'ETAS earthquake aftershock model tumor recurrence cancer dormancy point process'. PubMed: 0 results for 'self-exciting point process cancer recurrence Hawkes'. Semantic Scholar: 5 results for cross-field query — all tumor dormancy biology papers, zero seismology or point process references. Web search: Hawkes process Wikipedia mentions 'medicine' generically but no specific tumor recurrence papers found. One PNAS paper uses Hawkes processes for multicellular signaling (not recurrence).
candidates.related_but_distinct: Hawkes processes are used in: (1) neural spike train analysis, (2) multicellular signaling (PNAS 2021), (3) epidemic modeling. However, the ETAS model specifically (Omori-law decay + Gutenberg-Richter magnitude distribution + magnitude-dependent productivity + background rate) has NOT been applied to tumor dormancy/recurrence. The SPECIFIC seismological parameters (p-value, b-value, branching ratio, background rate) have no oncological analogs in published literature. Oncology uses exponential/Weibull recurrence models.
candidates.bridge_novelty: HIGH — ETAS parameter mapping (Omori p → paracrine decay, GR b-value → awakening magnitude distribution, branching ratio → subcritical/supercritical cascade threshold) is entirely novel. Note: Hawkes process exists in limited biomedical contexts but ETAS-specific framework has zero oncology presence.

candidates.title: Stochastic Resetting Optimization Predicts Optimal Antibiotic Pulsing for Persister Clearance
candidates.field_a: Statistical physics — stochastic resetting theory (Evans & Majumdar 2011)
candidates.field_c: Infectious disease microbiology — antibiotic persister clearance
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'stochastic resetting Evans Majumdar antibiotic persister bacteria first passage'. PubMed: 0 results for 'stochastic resetting biology optimization'. Semantic Scholar: Evans-Majumdar stochastic resetting papers (10 results) are pure physics/math with zero bacterial persistence references. Web search confirmed no paper connects Evans-Majumdar resetting framework to persister clearance.
candidates.related_but_distinct: IMPORTANT CAVEAT: Pulse dosing optimization for persister bacteria IS an active research area. Singh et al. 2022 (PLOS Comp Biol) developed systematic pulse dosing design using PK/PD models (on/off ratio optimization). Khan & Imran 2018 modeled optimal dosing against resistant bacteria. Himeoka & Mitarai 2019 analyzed optimal waking-up strategies. However, ALL use pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic or control theory frameworks — NONE use the Evans-Majumdar stochastic resetting optimization (optimal restart rate for first-passage time minimization). The novelty is in the SPECIFIC mathematical framework, not the problem domain.
candidates.bridge_novelty: MODERATE-HIGH — the problem (optimal pulse dosing for persisters) is studied, but the specific mathematical framework (stochastic resetting with optimal restart rate r*) provides new analytical predictions (r_optimal ~ sqrt(a*k)) not derivable from PK/PD models. Similar to S014 where TUR was novel even though bacterial growth variability was studied.

candidates.title: Soil Liquefaction Mechanics Explains the Osteoarthritis Cartilage Tipping Point
candidates.field_c: Orthopedic biomechanics — OA cartilage tipping point
candidates.evidence: PubMed: 0 results for 'soil liquefaction pore pressure cartilage osteoarthritis degeneration cyclic'. Semantic Scholar: 5 results for cross-field query — all soil liquefaction papers, zero cartilage/orthopedic references. Web search confirmed no papers connecting liquefaction theory to cartilage mechanics. Mow's biphasic cartilage theory (1980) describes fluid-saturated porous mechanics but never references liquefaction failure criteria (CSR threshold, N_liq, Seed's curves).
candidates.related_but_distinct: Biphasic/poroelastic models of cartilage (Mow, Ateshian groups) describe fluid flow in cartilage but focus on steady-state or monotonic loading. They do NOT model the cyclic-loading-specific FAILURE MODE that liquefaction theory captures (progressive pore pressure accumulation → effective stress loss → catastrophic failure). The liquefaction-specific parameters (CSR, N_liq, pore pressure ratio r_u) have no cartilage analogs in published literature.
candidates.bridge_novelty: HIGH — the cyclic stress ratio failure criterion and liquefaction resistance curves applied to cartilage is entirely novel

summary.recommendation_rationale: All 6 confirmed DISJOINT. Top 4 ranked by (confidence × impact × bridge_novelty): #4 (ETAS × tumor, highest confidence 8 + impact 8), #5 (resetting × persisters, impact 8, caveat: problem domain is active but framework is novel), #1 (Mpemba × aggregation, high novelty, impact 7), #2 (PLD2 × bacterial FMM, failed paradigm recycling, impact 7). Candidates #3 and #6 have lower confidence (6) but are valid DISJOINT targets.
